vocabulary.name: GoldRush / Covalent API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain terminology for Covalent GoldRush APIs covering blockchain data, balances, transactions, NFTs, pipelines, and Hyperliquid market data.

terms.label: Chain ID
terms.definition: Numeric identifier for an EVM-compatible blockchain network (e.g., 1 for Ethereum mainnet, 137 for Polygon, 8453 for Base). Used to disambiguate identical addresses across networks.

terms.label: Chain Name
terms.definition: Human-readable slug identifying a blockchain network (e.g., 'eth-mainnet', 'base-mainnet', 'polygon-mainnet'). Used as a path parameter in most GoldRush Foundational API endpoints.

terms.label: Wallet Address
terms.definition: A 0x-prefixed hex string identifying an Ethereum-style account on an EVM chain. ENS names, Lens handles, and Unstoppable Domain names are also accepted on the Base service resolve endpoint.

terms.label: Contract Address
terms.definition: A 0x-prefixed hex string identifying a deployed smart contract (e.g., an ERC20 token, NFT collection, or DEX pool) on an EVM chain.

terms.label: Token Balance
terms.definition: Raw on-chain token balance expressed as an integer string in the token's smallest denomination (e.g., wei for ETH, with 18 decimals). Divide by 10^contract_decimals to get a human-readable amount.

terms.label: Fiat Quote
terms.definition: The current USD (or selected quote_currency) value of a token holding, computed from the on-chain balance and the quote_rate spot price.

terms.label: Quote Rate
terms.definition: Spot price of one token unit in the requested quote currency (default USD). Null when the price feed is unavailable.

terms.label: Quote Currency
terms.definition: Three-letter fiat or crypto currency code used to express token values throughout a response (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'ETH'). Defaults to 'USD'.

terms.label: Contract Decimals
terms.definition: Number of decimal places the token contract uses. Used to convert the raw balance string to a human-readable float. Most ERC20 tokens use 18 decimals; USDC uses 6.

terms.label: Native Token
terms.definition: Boolean flag indicating the balance item represents the chain's native gas token (e.g., ETH on Ethereum, MATIC on Polygon) rather than a deployed ERC20 contract.

terms.label: Transaction Hash
terms.definition: 66-character hex string (0x + 64 hex digits) uniquely identifying a transaction on a given chain. Sometimes called a transaction ID (TXID).

terms.label: Block Height
terms.definition: Sequential integer identifying a block's position in a blockchain. Block 0 is the genesis block. Used as a point-in-time reference for historical balance and portfolio queries.

terms.label: Block Signed At
terms.definition: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp recorded by the block proposer when the block was finalized. May differ slightly from wall-clock time depending on the chain's block time.

terms.label: Gas Spent
terms.definition: Actual gas units consumed by a transaction (≤ gas_offered). Multiplied by gas_price to derive the total transaction fee.

terms.label: Gas Price
terms.definition: Price per gas unit in wei paid by the sender. Total fee = gas_spent × gas_price.

terms.label: Log Events
terms.definition: Array of decoded EVM event logs emitted during a transaction. Each entry includes the sender contract, raw topic hashes, raw data, and a decoded object with the ABI-decoded event name, signature, and parameters.

terms.label: Supports ERC
terms.definition: Array of token standard strings indicating which ERC interfaces the contract implements. Common values: 'erc20', 'erc721', 'erc1155'.

terms.label: NFT Data
terms.definition: Array of individual NFT token records present in an ERC-721 or ERC-1155 balance item, including token_id, token_balance, token_url, and external metadata.

terms.label: Topic Hash
terms.definition: Keccak-256 hash of an EVM event signature (e.g., Transfer(address,address,uint256)). Used to filter log events in the Base service log endpoints.

terms.label: Time Bucket
terms.definition: Integer index used in the bulk transactions endpoint representing a UTC-day window. Time bucket 0 = most recent day, 1 = the previous day, etc.

terms.label: Pipeline
terms.definition: A GoldRush Pipeline API resource that continuously streams decoded blockchain data from a source chain to a configured destination (ClickHouse, Kafka, S3, Postgres, SQS, or Webhook).

terms.label: Destination
terms.definition: The target data store or messaging system where a Pipeline delivers decoded blockchain events. Supported types: clickhouse, kafka, s3, gcs, r2, postgres, sqs, webhook.

terms.label: ABI Decoding
terms.definition: Process of translating raw EVM transaction input data and log event bytes into human-readable parameter names and values using a smart contract's Application Binary Interface (ABI).

terms.label: Service Key
terms.definition: Credential type used to authenticate programmatic access to the GoldRush Pipeline API. Distinct from the API key (Bearer token) used by the Foundational and other REST APIs.

terms.label: Bearer Token
terms.definition: GoldRush API key sent in the HTTP Authorization header as 'Bearer cqt_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx'. Minted in the GoldRush Platform dashboard. Required for all Foundational, Streaming, Hyperliquid, and x402 API calls.

terms.label: API Credits
terms.definition: Unit of consumption metering for GoldRush API calls. Each endpoint costs a defined number of credits per call. Monthly credit allotments vary by plan (Free: 25,000; Vibe Coding: 10,000; Professional: 300,000). Flex credits can be purchased beyond the allotment.

terms.label: OHLCV
terms.definition: Open-High-Low-Close-Volume candlestick data for a trading pair. Provided by the GoldRush Streaming API for DEX trading pairs and tokens in both one-shot query and live subscription form.

terms.label: DEX Pair
terms.definition: A liquidity pool or trading pair on a decentralized exchange (e.g., ETH/USDC on Uniswap). Identified by a pool contract address on a specific chain.

terms.label: WebSocket Subscription
terms.definition: A persistent GraphQL-over-WebSocket channel that pushes real-time events to the client. GoldRush Streaming API subscriptions include new DEX pairs, OHLCV updates, wallet activity, and Hyperliquid order book streams.

terms.label: Clearinghouse State
terms.definition: Hyperliquid perpetuals account snapshot including open positions, margin, leverage, and unrealized PnL for a given wallet address. Returned by the GoldRush Hyperliquid Info API clearinghouseState request type.

terms.label: HTTP 402 Payment Required
terms.definition: Protocol extension where an API endpoint returns HTTP 402 with payment metadata; the client (typically an AI agent) settles a micropayment on-chain and retries. GoldRush x402 endpoints enable per-request blockchain data access without a long-lived API key.

terms.label: Spot Clearinghouse State
terms.definition: Hyperliquid spot market account snapshot showing token balances held in the clearinghouse for a user address. Returned by the spotClearinghouseState request type in the GoldRush Hyperliquid Info API.

terms.label: L2 Order Book
terms.definition: Level-2 aggregated order book snapshot showing price levels and total size at each level for a Hyperliquid market. Wire-compatible with the public Hyperliquid feed; delivered via the GoldRush Hyperliquid WebSocket API.

terms.label: L2 Order Book Diff
terms.definition: GoldRush-exclusive differential update channel for L2 order books, sending only changed price levels rather than full snapshots to reduce bandwidth.

terms.label: L4 Order Book
terms.definition: GoldRush-exclusive order-level view of a Hyperliquid market showing individual resting orders with their sizes and order IDs, enabling more granular order-flow analysis than L2.

terms.label: HIP-3
terms.definition: Hyperliquid Improvement Proposal 3, defining programmatic native token creation (spot markets for new tokens). GoldRush covers HIP-3 outcome markets in both the Info and WebSocket APIs.

terms.label: HIP-4
terms.definition: Hyperliquid Improvement Proposal 4, defining prediction / outcome markets on Hyperliquid. Accessible via the GoldRush Hyperliquid Info and WebSocket APIs with dedicated outcomeMeta and outcome market stream endpoints.

terms.label: Cross-Chain Activity
terms.definition: GoldRush endpoint family that locates all chains where a given wallet address has had on-chain activity in a single API call, returning chain names, chain IDs, and the most recent activity timestamp per chain.

terms.label: Token Approval
terms.definition: An ERC20 or NFT allowance granted by a wallet to a spender contract, enabling the spender to transfer tokens on the owner's behalf. GoldRush Security Service surfaces outstanding approvals and risk exposure.

terms.label: Foundational Chain Support
terms.definition: The highest tier of GoldRush chain support, providing real-time indexing, full historical data, and SLA guarantees. Foundational chains: Base, BNB Smart Chain, Ethereum, Gnosis, Optimism, Polygon.

terms.label: Frontier Chain Support
terms.definition: Second tier of GoldRush chain support for newer or lower-volume networks. Includes Arbitrum, Avalanche, Bitcoin, Berachain, Solana, Monad, MegaETH, zkSync Era, and others.
